id-1770047083023-988.title: From Clicks to OPA5 Tests: AI-Powered Test Generation for Fiori Apps
id-1770047083023-988.description:
Test automation in UI5 is powerful but expensive: writing and maintaining OPA5 tests requires deep technical skills, a lot of time, and constant refactoring when the UI changes. Our goal was to remove this barrier completely and enable teams to transform real user behavior into automated tests – without writing code.

That is why we built AI-powered test generation for Fiori Apps: to drastically reduce test creation time, enable non-developers to contribute, and let experts focus on quality, architecture, and innovation instead of repetitive scripting.

This spottalk explains the motivation behind the AI approach, how the first experimental version works, and what it means for the future of UI5 quality assurance.

speakers.bio:
I'm a software engineer specialized on machine learning and AI, with roots in SAPUI5 frontend development as well as in usability and UX, which continues to shape how I approach technology.

At Simplifier AG, I build AI-powered features for our low-code platform, helping customers accelerate application development and solve complex challenges more efficiently.
A particular focus of my work is applying AI to real-world scenarios and building tools that developers and customers actually enjoy using.

When I'm not at work, you'll usually find me swapping my keyboard for a microphone, singing and writing songs with my indie rock band.

id-1770048155302-500.title: Taking UI5 to the Next Level with AI
id-1770048155302-500.description:
AI is transforming how we build UI5 applications. In this deep dive, I'll share my hands-on experience with AI across the entire UI5 development lifecycle and show you what our future looks like.

First, we'll explore AI-driven development tools. From GitHub Copilot to Claude Code with MCP servers (including UI5's own MCP server), I'll demonstrate how these tools are changing our workflow. The result? My most ambitious side projects, with the least manual coding ever.

But why stop at AI helping us write code? Why not generate the UI directly at runtime? By combining CAP with AI, we can generate complete UI5 views - forms and tables - on the fly based on entity metadata, with server-side pre-rendering for instant delivery. Could this be how we build UIs in the future?

And while we wait for that future, let's bring AI into our UI5 apps today. We'll discuss practical enhancements for end users: text optimizers for long-form input, smart filters like SAP's Fiori Elements approach and more.

After this session, you'll have practical insights on using AI tools effectively, inspiration for AI-generated UIs, and concrete ideas to bring AI features into your own UI5 apps. 

The question isn't whether AI will change UI5 development - it already has. The question is: are you ready to make the most of it?

speakers.bio:
Wouter Lemaire is an independent SAP solution architect, developer & consultant. From ABAP WebDynpro to UI5 and from BTP to AI, he's always been an early adopter of SAP innovation.

Today he works with CAP, UI5, ABAP, and AI, building agents with SAP AI Core and Joule, and connecting SAP systems to platforms like Claude and Microsoft Copilot via MCP servers and OData APIs.

Wouter is an active SAP Community member, regular speaker at UI5con, reCAP and SAP TechEd and co-organizer of SAP Inside Track Belgium.

id-1770113621347-702.title: What's New in the Models Area
id-1770113621347-702.description: In this session, we will show new features provided for all models of UI5 as well as features specifically provided for the OData V4 model. These features include bound filters, the negation in Filters, support for Edm.Decimal with scale="floating" and in that context OData V4.01, the usage of $count on a collection valued navigation property in lists or how to use the preference ContinueOnError with OData V4. We will also provide an outlook on combining data aggregation and editing.

speakers.bio: I'm a Senior Developer in SAPUI5 Core mainly focused on the databinding layer.
Development architect of the team behind UI5's V4 OData model. Enjoys cycling.
Development Manager in SAPUI5 development focusing on Models, Formatters, Types, ...

id-1770772093152-284.title: How to use n8n for agentic integrations and cool insights
id-1770772093152-284.description: In this talk I would like to present ways to integrate agentic insights into your Fiori app. Not only will I show you how to let agents do the OCR on an invoice, I will also show you how to use it to get insights into the relevance of the invoice, or how to interpret an image and get it to gauge the relevance of the attachment to your business object. Your users are going to love having quick insights in uploaded documents without having to open everything one by one and judging it themselves. I will be using n8n as the task runner for this.

speakers.bio: Hi I'm Jorg! I like software, it's my hobby and my profession. Professionally it's anything SAP end Enterprise related but always web or cloud oriented. Having completely ignored the no code / low code trends, I am interested in the rise of the LLM and agentic processing. As for hobbies, it's kind of the same! Except then I use everything except SAP.

Over the past decade, I’ve architected and built SAP‑based platforms for government, enterprise, and global clients — from modernising legacy systems to developing full‑stack BTP applications, integration patterns, and clean‑core cloud solutions. I’m known for bridging the gap between business and engineering, communicating complex ideas simply, and guiding teams through high‑stakes technical decisions.

When I’m not designing architectures, you’ll find me contributing to the SAP community, running events like SAP CodeJam Brisbane, and helping teams level up with modern technologies.

I love a good beer & I'm known to ride bikes down mountains too

id-1771252496595-789.title: UI5 Linter in Action: Integrating It Into Your Development Process
id-1771252496595-789.description:
Modernize a legacy UI5 app end-to-end using UI5 linter — from your first ui5lint report to a clean, CSP-compliant, future-proof codebase. You'll auto-fix what you can, manually resolve the rest, and wire linting into your workflow and CI so regressions never sneak back in.

Prerequisites: Laptop with Node.js (LTS) and Git installed, a code editor, and basic UI5 development experience.

speakers.bio: Matthias is a principal software architect at SAP, where he has been working on the UI5 framework for more than a decade. He is passionate about developer tooling and open source, maintaining projects like UI5 CLI, UI5 linter, and UI5 MCP server. He is driven by a constant desire to improve the developer experience for the UI5 community.
Hi, I'm Max and I love to support open-source projects. With my team, I build and maintain tools to improve the developer experience while mostly focusing on the UI5 CLI and UI5 Linter projects.

id-1771253244756-803.title: UI5 Tools - Update 2026
id-1771253244756-803.description:
In this session we will give a large update about all the different tools around UI5 introduced newly since last UI5con or got significant new features.
- UI5 MCP Server: This tool was released in late summer 2025 to offer LLMs UI5 expertise. When using AI when developing UI5 projects the UI5 MCP server is a must have for developers. We will give a short introduction in this new tool and show new features added since the UI5ers live session covered this tool and how easy it is to migrate legacy projects to the latest best practices.
- UI5 CLI: On UI5con 2025, we gave a preview on features to come for UI5 CLI. Over the last year, the team made significant progress on major updates for UI5 CLI (component type, incremental build and many more). Therefore, we would like to share further updates with the community.

speakers.bio: Florian is the Product Manager overseeing UI5 Tools and the foundational building blocks of UI5 Core. He takes care of UI5 CLI, UI5 linter, UI5 plugins for coding agents and many other tools making your life as an UI5 developer easier.

id-1771413618172-951.title: TypedJSONModel: Closing the Gap Between UI5 and TypeScript
id-1771413618172-951.description:
TypeScript has firmly arrived in the UI5 ecosystem. However, parts of the traditional model APIs are still rooted in untyped JavaScript patterns, which can lead to runtime errors and limited tooling support. This talk introduces TypedJSONModel, a strongly typed extension of JSONModel that enhances type safety and developer experience in UI5 projects.
 
TypedJSONModel wraps the existing JSONModel with types, enabling compile-time validation and rich IDE support such as autocompletion and type inference. It helps catch invalid paths and type mismatches before the application runs.
 
The session highlights practical usage patterns and shows how TypedJSONModel reduces subtle bugs and supports safer refactoring in UI5 applications.

speakers.bio:
Hello there, I’m Martin Broede 👋
My journey into the IT sector has been quite diverse — from spending years traveling across Europe as a freelance musician to becoming a software developer in the cybersecurity field. During the COVID pandemic, I changed careers and joined a software company specializing in e-government solutions, where I gained hands-on development experience. Later, I deepened my knowledge by completing vocational training in Business Informatics at SAP. Today, I work as a full-stack developer at Sedevo, where we provide cybersecurity consulting and build software for cyber threat detection, among other solutions.
I am senior developer at SAP in one of the UI5 teams and am looking forward to talking about our latest inventions.

id-1772617793135-401.title: Metadata-Driven Test Generation for SAP Fiori Elements
id-1772617793135-401.description:
Writing comprehensive OPA5 integration tests for SAP Fiori elements applications has historically been a manual, time-consuming process, as standard generators typically provide only a "hello world" test scaffold.
By leveraging the SAP Fiori elements test library and OData service metadata, we can now bridge this gap by automatically generating OPA5 tests that cover the specific features enabled by your metadata out of the box.

This session introduces a new approach to test automation where the initial heavy lifting is handled by SAP Fiori tools, allowing developers to focus their efforts on testing unique business logic and custom extensions. We will explore how metadata-driven generation ensures higher test coverage from day one while maintaining the flexibility to layer on manual enhancements.

Join us to see how you can slash your testing overhead and move from empty test files to fully verified applications in a fraction of the time.

speakers.bio:
I'm a development expert who has shifted from crafting business applications to building SAP Fiori tools.
My passion lies in UI/UX design, and my technical sweet spot is cloud-native apps and middlewares, primarily using TypeScript and JavaScript.

You can also find me contributing to the UI5 community or spoiling my cats.

id-1772792155148-830.title: Offline capabilities in UI5 - Let's discuss
id-1772792155148-830.description:
In this session we want to show you some of our solution ideas we have evaluated regarding offline functionalities. We’d also love to have a discussion with you. We want to hear your opinions or scenarios when it comes to offline modes in your UI5 applications.
Join us and share your experiences and expectations with us.

speakers.bio: I am senior developer at SAP in one of the UI5 teams and am looking forward to talking about our latest inventions.
UI5 Core Developer

id-1772937781051-707.title: UI5 has entered the chat – AI, MCP Apps and UI5
id-1772937781051-707.description:
The way users interact with software is changing and AI is reshaping not just how we build apps, but how people experience them.
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) bridges this gap on a technical level, giving AI hosts a standardized way to connect to backend data and services. With MCP Apps, this now extends to UI and UX too: forms, dashboards, and workflows rendering directly inside AI hosts like Claude, VS Code Copilot, or SAP Joule.
In this session, we look at what this means for UI5 developers. We'll examine Joule's current approach to surfacing business UI, its benefits and limitations, and explore why MCP Apps might offer a more flexible path forward. Because UI5 Web Components are built on web standards, they are a natural fit for this new world: rich, interactive business UIs inside AI conversations, backed by the same services that power our Fiori apps today.

speakers.company: Independent SAP Solution Architect, Developer and Consultant
speakers.bio:
With over a decade of experience in the SAP ecosystem, I’ve worked across roles in Solution Architecture, Development, and Consulting, with a strong focus on modern SAP technologies such as SAP BTP, UI5, the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), RAP, and native SAP HANA. 

Outside of work, I’m a proud husband and father of three wonderful kids.
Independent SAP Developer & Open Source Enthusiast

id-1773215009302-635.title: One CAP Core, 50 UI5 Apps: Digitizing Construction Sites with UI5
id-1773215009302-635.description:
What happens when you want to digitize construction site processes end to end instead of building isolated apps? In this talk, I will show how we built and run a software solution with more than 40 Fiori elements apps for master data and administration, plus 10 freestyle UI5 process apps for operational workflows, all on top of one SAP CAP backend.

The business goal behind this landscape is clear: digitizing construction sites, warehouse interactions, article movements, returns, picking, and settlement processes in one coherent platform. The session focuses on the architectural decisions and trade-offs behind that setup: where Fiori elements gave us speed and consistency, where freestyle UI5 was the better fit, and how we kept both worlds aligned through shared models, a reusable UI5 library, OData V4, MDC components, common error handling, personalization, and a coherent deployment model on SAP BTP.

Instead of theory, this is a real project story from the construction industry, with concrete patterns, pitfalls, and lessons learned from scaling UI5 in an enterprise environment. Attendees will leave with practical guidance for structuring larger software solutions that connect back office administration with field and site processes, without ending up with duplicated code, fragmented UX, or an unmaintainable app portfolio.

speakers.company: cbs Corporate Business Solutions Unternehmensberatung GmbH
speakers.bio: I'm a Solution Architect specializing in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Fiori development. I focus on designing scalable architectures and leveraging modern tooling — including AI-assisted development workflows — to streamline SAP UI5/Fiori projects.

speakers.company: cbs Corporate Business Solutions Unternehmensberatung GmbH
speakers.bio:
I am a full stack developer that has no greater joy than solving problems und understanding stuff (especially if its things that are "not relevant" - tell that to my brain at 3am). No matter if its HANA native or the depths of UI5: If its code, I want to know how it works.
Besides that I like to create small dev tools that make the lives of other (hopefully) better.

id-1773224478176-950.title: How does Flex integrate into UI5 and what could possibly go wrong?!
id-1773224478176-950.description: Ever wondered how UI5 Flexibility works under the hood? In this session, we will give an overview how Flexibility is built into the UI5 framework and talk about some key principles on how UI5 Flexibility works, deep-diving into some parts of the UI5 framework that are crucial for Flexibility. We will also give some examples on how it could break and how to avoid that.

speakers.bio: I am a Architect in the UI5 Flexibility Area, focusing on the frontend.

I’m a developer working in the field of UI5 Flexibility, where I focus on making enterprise software more adaptable and user-friendly. I genuinely enjoy software development—not just professionally, but also as a hobby, especially exploring what’s possible with AI. Alongside my work, I also co-run a nonprofit kindergarten with my wife on a volunteer basis, which is a big part of my life. When I’m not coding or organizing things, I like to spend quality time with my family and step away from the screen.

id-1773319557239-724.title: Unlock Your App’s Accessibility: Hands‑On with UI5, Tools & AI
id-1773319557239-724.description:
This hands-on workshop guides developers through practical accessibility fixes on a pre-provided UI5 application using official UI5 documentation, accessibility tools, and AI-powered assistants. Participants will work in small groups or individually to identify real accessibility issues (semantic markup, ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, focus management, color contrast, and screen reader behavior) and apply concrete fixes. The session begins with a short orientation to core accessibility principles and the UI5 accessibility features, followed by a structured troubleshooting workflow: reproduce the issue, consult UI5 docs and best practices, run automated and manual tests (axe, Lighthouse, NVDA/VoiceOver), and implement fixes. Throughout, we’ll demonstrate how AI can accelerate discovery and remediation—suggesting code changes. Attendees will leave confident in how to handle common accessibility issues: how to reproduce and diagnose problems, where to search authoritative guidance (UI5 docs, WAI-ARIA, WCAG, testing tool docs), which tools and techniques to use (automated scanners, screen readers, keyboard testing, AI). 

⚠️ Please come prepared!
 This is a hands-on session — to make the most of your time with us, please
 complete the prerequisites before the workshop. Setup instructions and the
 pre-requirements are in our repo:
 👉 https://github.com/SAP-samples/ui5con-2026-a11y-hands-on/blob/main/hands-on-lectures/prerequisites.md

speakers.bio: I’m passionate about sports and the energy, discipline, and teamwork they bring into everyday life. I also care deeply about accessibility, because creating inclusive environments means opening up opportunities for everyone to participate, grow, and succeed.

I’m a developer and accessibility specialist focused on making the web open to everyone while keeping things agile as a Scrum Master. I bring the same high energy to my work that I do to my favorite extreme sports, always looking for the next big adventure. I’m excited to be at UI5con to chat about building more accessible tech and sharing adventure stories!
Passionate about Accessibility and building inclusive digital experiences. As the UI5 Accessibility Lead Product Expert, I'm honored to help make UI5 one of the most accessible front-end frameworks.

id-1773393354498-840.title: Field Report: Freestyle UI5 + SAP Fiori elements building blocks
id-1773393354498-840.description:
In a recent large full-stack project built with UI5 and CAP, we implemented a hybrid UI approach: freestyle UI5 as the baseline, combined with SAP Fiori elements building blocks (flexible programming model). This differs from the more common approach of using SAP Fiori elements as the baseline, using custom pages and sections where needed.

This field report will answer the following questions:
When should I go freestyle UI5 first? When Fiori elements first?
What application structure is required for each approach?
Which tools and documentation can I use?

speakers.bio: Nico focuses on web development technologies and is interested in all things SAP Business Technology Platform. Before joining the Developer Advocates, he studied Psychology and therefore likes to focus on the user as well as the developer experience.

id-1773397554412-119.title: What's New: SAP Fiori Elements V4, Runtime AI & My Home
id-1773397554412-119.description: Learn what’s new in SAP Fiori elements V4, Runtime AI features, and My Home. See how annotation-driven UIs accelerate app delivery while full flexibility for the target picture. Explore the latest news around My Home as a personalized launchpad where insight cards surface KPIs, tasks, and recommendations. Experience our latest Runtime AI features to elevate your workflow with smart helpers.

speakers.bio: As the Lead Product Manager for SAP Fiori, my focus is all about evolving the developer experience—from streamlining our core tools to exploring the next frontier of AI-driven application design. There's nothing quite like the energy of the UI5 community, and I'm incredibly excited to connect with you all at my first in-person UI5con!

id-1773398080104-255.title: Flexibility at Scale with Freestyle SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori Elements
id-1773398080104-255.description:
This hands-on workshop demonstrates how to build flexible, enterprise-grade user interfaces by combining freestyle SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements technologies. Participants work with a CAP backend based on the SFLIGHT sample service to construct a travel dashboard featuring object-centric design.

Prerequisites: Laptop, being able to login to SAP Business Application Studio via provided workshop-account, alternatively local installation of VS Code, Git, and latest SAP Fiori tools.

speakers.bio: Lead Product Manager SAP Fiori tools

As the Lead Product Manager for SAP Fiori, my focus is all about evolving the developer experience—from streamlining our core tools to exploring the next frontier of AI-driven application design. There's nothing quite like the energy of the UI5 community, and I'm incredibly excited to connect with you all at my first in-person UI5con!

id-1773399990485-307.title: Beyond Controls: Extending UI5 with 3rd-Party Libraries and Web Components
id-1773399990485-307.description:
UI5 is often thought of mainly as a control library, but it’s much more than that: It's a very flexible framework that can be extended and used with many different technologies. In this demo, we show how a UI5 application can integrate third-party libraries and external web components.

Using a simple supermarket app as an example, we integrate Three.js into a custom UI5 control to render a 3D model of the supermarket and guide customers to a product’s location. We also include a custom web component that connects to a Bluetooth printer and prints directions to the selected product.

The demo illustrates how UI5 can easily use npm packages, modern tooling, and reusable web components, making it possible to extend UI5 applications beyond the standard UI controls.

speakers.bio: Nico focuses on web development technologies and is interested in all things SAP Business Technology Platform. Before joining the Developer Advocates, he studied Psychology and therefore likes to focus on the user as well as the developer experience.

id-1773419895916-135.title: Honey, I Shrunk the Boilerplate: FLP Sandbox 2.0
id-1773419895916-135.description:
Testing SAP Fiori applications locally often requires dealing with the FLP Sandbox - a solution that has grown organically over the years. Developers frequently struggle with complex, SAP-internal configuration requirements, copy-pasted boilerplate code, and sparse documentation. With UI5 2.x introducing significant API changes, many of these patterns have become deprecated, forcing both the FLP and developers to adapt.

This session introduces the completely redesigned FLP Sandbox 2.0 - a modern, stable, and easy-to-use solution for local Fiori application development and testing.

What you will learn:
- Why a new sandbox was necessary and the core principles behind its design
- The new declarative configuration approach that works out-of-the-box with minimal setup
- High-level architecture including the boot manifest mechanism
- How the sandbox supports both UI5 1.x and 2.x through a unified bootstrap

The highlight of this session is a live migration demonstration, where we transform existing applications from the legacy sandbox to the new approach.

Whether you are new to Fiori development or a seasoned UI5 developer preparing for the 2.x migration, this session provides practical guidance to streamline your local development workflow. You will leave with a clear understanding of how to adopt the new sandbox and reduce technical debt in your projects.

speakers.bio:
I've been with SAP for over 10 years, starting out as an application developer building UI5-based apps. For the past six years, I've been working on the UI5 Core Framework itself — deep in the engine room of sap.ui.core, shaping the fundamentals that thousands of developers rely on every day.

What drives me is the continuous renewal of UI5: keeping it modern, relevant, and genuinely enjoyable to build with. I care deeply about developer experience, and I believe that a great framework should feel invisible — powerful enough to handle anything, but never getting in your way.

At this year's UI5con, I'll be sharing what we've been building around the next generation of the FLP Sandbox — a topic close to my heart and the result of a lot of iteration, collaboration, and passion for getting the tooling right.

I'm a development expert who has shifted from crafting business applications to building SAP Fiori tools.
My passion lies in UI/UX design, and my technical sweet spot is cloud-native apps and middlewares, primarily using TypeScript and JavaScript.

You can also find me contributing to the UI5 community or spoiling my cats.

id-1773435568849-361.title: Supercharge UI5 apps with Chrome’s Built-in AI
id-1773435568849-361.description: Chrome's built-in AI is here, it's free, and it runs right in the browser. In this fast-paced session, we'll kick things off by showing you how to quickly scaffold and build a UI5 app using AI-powered tools and modern dev workflows — so you spend less time on boilerplate and more time on what matters. Then we'll dive into Chrome's AI APIs — Prompt, Summarization, Translation, and more — and wire them directly into your app to bring it to life with real intelligence. No backend. No API key. Just you, your browser, and some seriously smart UI5 components.

speakers.bio:
Front-end engineer for over a decade, still having fun with it. I build fast, clean UIs and spend a good chunk of time figuring out how AI can actually improve real products and push them beyond the hype. I like breaking things almost as much as fixing them.

Currently an architect on the UI5 and UI5 Web Components team in Sofia.

Outside of tech, you’ll likely find me running, lifting, or chasing snow in the mountains.

I’m a front-end engineer with almost 15 years of experience, currently focused on SAPUI5 control development.

A big part of my work is around Smart Controls and building with a strong focus on performance. I care about how controls behave under the hood—rendering, data binding, and the small implementation details that can make a big difference in responsiveness and scalability.

Most of my experience is deep in the UI5 stack, optimizing controls and making sure they stay fast and efficient in complex enterprise scenarios.

Lately, I’ve also been exploring how AI can be used in a practical way to support day-to-day development, but without losing focus on solid engineering fundamentals.

I'm leading the product management team for SAP UI Technologies and I'm a member of the SAPUI5 team from the very beginning. I'm looking forward to another great episode of UI5con together with all of you!

From day one of the Phoenix project — now known as UI5/OpenUI5/SAPUI5 — to today as its Chief Architect, I've spent my career making UI5 modern, faster, leaner, and more fun to build with. My favorite part of the job? Meeting the developers actually using it. See you at UI5con!

id-1742298313676-509.title: Closing
id-1742298313676-509.description: Wrap up UI5con with final announcements and a look ahead to the future.

speakers.bio: VP of UI Technology Development at SAP and a regular speaker at UI5 related conferences.

id-1743582599089-979.description: Officially kick off UI5con with us! Join the opening session for welcome remarks, an overview of the event, and a look ahead at what's in store.
